response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced senior software engineer with a broad background in web, TypeScript, Node.js, and some embedded work, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language skills — native-level Japanese is a hard, non-negotiable requirement. Beyond that, his experience is overwhelmingly in web and application-layer development; he lacks the C/Linux kernel depth, security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, cryptographic protocols), and Layer 2/3 networking expertise the role demands. His brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and the Sound Position Server project are too narrow and dated to bridge the gap.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and systems contexts demonstrates strong general engineering capability
C and C++ experience at National Instruments and in personal projects shows some familiarity with low-level, non-GC languages relevant to embedded development
Strong English proficiency satisfies the English documentation and email communication requirement
concerns: No Japanese language skills whatsoever — native-level Japanese is listed as a mandatory requirement and is a hard disqualifier
No security domain experience: zero evidence of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device authentication, encryption protocols, or Linux kernel vulnerability countermeasures
Primary tech stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web; C programming and Linux kernel modification experience are minimal and not recent

key_factors.factor: No security or networking domain knowledge
